ops.body: cc-steer v0.14.0 SHIPPED (2026-07-17 ~09:30Z): the T3.1 eval-what-you-serve stack is on PyPI. Sequence: the operator had moved main (9 chore commits: prek/docs-CI/guides) diverging from the v0.13.0 base, so the 5-commit stack was rebased onto their tip (diffstats byte-identical to the originals), verified in an ISOLATED venv (never touching the live retrain's .venv), pushed as 745a608e. CI then caught a real break my local gates couldn't: the test job runs WITHOUT the retrain extra, and T3.1's diagnostic wrapper imported tinker for its catch tuple — fixed as d0bc7fe6 (TinkerCallError(RuntimeError) domain conversion at the score_rows_tinker boundary; wrapper catches (OSError, RuntimeError), no tinker import; pattern recorded as cc-notes note 70f8dd62 'Wrap SDK errors at optional-dependency boundaries'; reproduced + verified in a no-extra venv). CI 7/7 green → tag v0.14.0 → release-pypi green → PyPI latest 0.14.0 → cold-cache uvx smoke resolves, installs, runs (the first smoke hit the known stale-index/negative-cache pattern; --no-cache after propagation cleared it, same as v0.13.0). Plists unchanged (no flag changes since C1) — the Sunday 04:00 weekly lane and the nightly now run the NEW gate from PyPI automatically. Remaining for T3: the in-flight manual watcher retrain (13h+, Tinker shared-org slow lane, step1172 checkpoint seen, requests flowing) → promote under the served-frame gate → daemon serves → criterion f7c1abdd. C2 dispatches after the retrain exits (venv-swap hazard); its athome pin target is now >=0.5.1.
